recent_evidence.summary: Authored an explicit CPMG N=8 weak-13C discriminator model/intent for reimage1804_c02 and materialized it after verifier/advisory/queue gates. Intent verifier JSON verdict was verified with no hard errors/blockers/warnings; queue was idle before enqueue. Single-submit advisory ok=true with no blockers, 37 tau points, 12 x 50000 reps, estimated 536.2 s per average under the active 900 s nighttime cap. Bridge job nv23_cpmg_20260515_072306_auto_cpmg is running at average 1/12 with Final about 42.714 kcps, monitor active/no last_error, stop_requested=false, queued/staging empty. This touched the bridge queue; do not submit/mutate further bridge jobs until terminal/anomaly evidence.

Bridge-free one-average autosave sanity review of running CPMG N=8 job nv23_cpmg_20260515_072306_auto_cpmg. Raw export verified CPMG.xml, tau 0.45..1.60 us, 37 points, one saved average x 50000 reps, snake scan order, and do_adiabatic_inversion=0. The bridge remained running with Final about 44.318 kcps, monitor active/no last_error, stop_requested=false. Readout count/roles match XML expectation: readout1 true-0 reference, readout2 pi/ms=1 reference, readout3 final CPMG echo candidate signal. Readout3/self-baseline at nearest target taus was 0.9995 near 0.642 us and 0.9839 near 1.312 us, i.e. no obvious target-tau dip in this single 50k-shot average. Readout2 reference had a high point near 1.312 us, so terminal review must compare raw/reference/self-baseline views. No 13C claim or rejection is made from this nonterminal one-average subset.

Bridge-free two-average autosave sanity review of running CPMG N=8 job nv23_cpmg_20260515_072306_auto_cpmg. The bridge remained running/healthy at 2/12 averages with Final about 43.000 kcps, monitor active/no last_error, stop_requested=false, and no queue mutation. Raw export verified CPMG.xml, tau 0.45..1.60 us, 37 points, two saved averages x 50000 reps, snake scan order, and do_adiabatic_inversion=0. Scan-order-aware drift used Scan.ScanOrderEachAvg / snake mode and found no flagged averages. Combined readout3/self-baseline was 1.0197 near the 0.650 us target and 0.9822 near the 1.300 us target; the two averages disagree locally enough that this remains a nonterminal sanity check, not evidence for or against 13C.

last_next_action.agent_prompt: Treat this as an event-driven research wake. Read NV_RESEARCH_MEMORY.md startup memory first, then brief.md, human_advice.md, work/state.md, and compact project state. Use the Memory Index to read relevant sections from NV_RESEARCH_KNOWLEDGE.md only as needed. Read .manager/evidence.jsonl, project policy, detailed backlog entries, and directly relevant evidence only as needed, then use the same-wake runnable work loop to complete, update, or queue all currently runnable safe project work until a real stop condition is reached. Python should stay limited to state, queue safety, audit logs, and hard boundaries; the agent should own the scientific plan. Process advice inbox messages with list-advice-inbox/dispose-advice when present, record evidence, update backlog, write work/state.md, log.md, and work/notes files as useful, update NV_RESEARCH_KNOWLEDGE.md for reusable detailed lessons, and write the completion marker.

last_human_request.preview: Objective: Find a magnetic-field-aligned NV from image172647, then obtain a well-supported T2star and 13C conclusion. Start a project using the spatial range of <MATLAB_23C_ROOT>\SavedImages\3DXYZ-Image-2026-05-14-172647.mat as the initial search region. Identify an NV aligned to the magnetic field within that image range; if none is found, search different regions. Judge magnetic-field alignment using a strong-pi pulsed ODMR scan; if it does not show a clear usable resonance, reject
